user.title: AI Camera Centre
user.description: Global settings — you can change these later, and add cameras and alert targets from the integration page once setup finishes.

data.ai_task_entity: AI Task entity (blank = Home Assistant's default)
data.alarm_panel_entity: Alarm panel (optional — enables the 'armed' notify conditions and Alarmo triggering)
data.min_log_score: Minimum score to log an alert (1 = log every real alert)
data.repeat_context_minutes: Recent-activity context window in minutes (0 = off) — lets the AI spot the same person returning or loitering
data.log_window_start: Only log alerts after this time (optional)
data.log_window_end: Only log alerts before this time (optional)
data.alarmo_enabled: Trigger Alarmo on high-risk alerts (only while the panel is armed)
data.alarmo_trigger_score: Score at or above which Alarmo is triggered
data.process_presence: Process motion based on presence (house default)
data.process_armed: Process motion based on alarm state (house default)
data.process_time_mode: Process motion based on time (house default)
data.process_time_start: Custom window start (used when 'Between two times' is selected)
data.process_time_end: Custom window end (used when 'Between two times' is selected)
data.sun_entity: Sun entity used for day/night (default sun.sun)
data.response_style: AI response style / personality (optional — e.g. "in the style of Samuel L. Jackson"; affects wording only, never the score)

abort.single_instance_allowed: AI Camera Centre is already configured.

init.title: AI Camera Centre settings
init.description: The 'armed' notify conditions and Alarmo triggering both need an alarm panel selected. Time-window logging only records alerts within the chosen hours (leave blank to log at any time). The 'Process motion' rules are the house-wide default for skipping AI analysis entirely (presence AND alarm AND time must all allow it); each camera can follow this or set its own. Add or edit cameras and alert targets from the integration page.

data.ai_task_entity: AI Task entity (blank = Home Assistant's default)
data.alarm_panel_entity: Alarm panel (optional — enables the 'armed' notify conditions and Alarmo triggering)
data.min_log_score: Minimum score to log an alert (1 = log every real alert)
data.repeat_context_minutes: Recent-activity context window in minutes (0 = off) — lets the AI spot the same person returning or loitering
data.log_window_start: Only log alerts after this time (optional)
data.log_window_end: Only log alerts before this time (optional)
data.alarmo_enabled: Trigger Alarmo on high-risk alerts (only while the panel is armed)
data.alarmo_trigger_score: Score at or above which Alarmo is triggered
data.process_presence: Process motion based on presence (house default)
data.process_armed: Process motion based on alarm state (house default)
data.process_time_mode: Process motion based on time (house default)
data.process_time_start: Custom window start (used when 'Between two times' is selected)
data.process_time_end: Custom window end (used when 'Between two times' is selected)
data.sun_entity: Sun entity used for day/night (default sun.sun)
data.response_style: AI response style / personality (optional — e.g. "in the style of Samuel L. Jackson"; affects wording only, never the score)

user.title: Add camera
user.description: Any of the motion triggers turning on starts the AI analysis. The scene context is added to the AI prompt — describe what the camera sees, and say explicitly whether a gate is visible.

data.name: Camera name
data.motion_entities: Motion triggers (optional — leave blank to trigger via the ai_camera_centre.analyze service or the Analyze button)
data.motion_policy: Motion processing policy (follow the house settings, or set custom rules below)
data.process_presence: Custom: process motion based on presence
data.process_armed: Custom: process motion based on alarm state
data.process_time_mode: Custom: process motion based on time
data.process_time_start: Custom window start (used when 'Between two times' is selected)
data.process_time_end: Custom window end (used when 'Between two times' is selected)

reconfigure.title: Edit camera
reconfigure.description: Renaming keeps the camera's existing alert history. Any of the motion triggers turning on starts the AI analysis.

data.name: Camera name
data.motion_entities: Motion triggers (optional — leave blank to trigger via the ai_camera_centre.analyze service or the Analyze button)
data.motion_policy: Motion processing policy (follow the house settings, or set custom rules below)
data.process_presence: Custom: process motion based on presence
data.process_armed: Custom: process motion based on alarm state
data.process_time_mode: Custom: process motion based on time
data.process_time_start: Custom window start (used when 'Between two times' is selected)
data.process_time_end: Custom window end (used when 'Between two times' is selected)

error.invalid_name: Please enter a valid camera name.
error.duplicate_camera: A camera with this name already exists.

user.title: Add alert target
user.description: An alert target is a notify service that receives camera alerts. Set the minimum suspicion score it should be woken for, when it should be notified (presence / alarm state), and optionally restrict it to specific cameras (none selected = all cameras).

data.name: Name (optional — a friendly label, e.g. "Ben's phone")
data.min_score: Minimum suspicion score to notify (1 = every alert)
data.cameras: Only alert for these cameras (blank = all)

reconfigure.title: Edit alert target
reconfigure.description: Set the minimum suspicion score this target should be woken for, when it should be notified (presence / alarm state), and optionally restrict it to specific cameras (none selected = all cameras).

data.name: Name (optional — a friendly label, e.g. "Ben's phone")
data.min_score: Minimum suspicion score to notify (1 = every alert)
data.cameras: Only alert for these cameras (blank = all)

error.invalid_service: Please select or enter a notify service.

user.title: Add known visitor
user.description: Describe a household member or regular so the AI can recognise them and treat them as low-risk. The description is added to every camera's prompt — include distinguishing features (build, typical clothing, whether they use a wheelchair, a pet, etc.).

reconfigure.title: Edit known visitor
reconfigure.description: The description is added to every camera's prompt — include distinguishing features (build, typical clothing, a pet, etc.).

error.duplicate_visitor: A known person with this name already exists.
